claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API currently supports external research/fetch evidence with proper scope separation, and project facts require project-scoped path evidence.

evidence.summary: Valid evidence scopes are defined as project, external, user, or unknown
evidence.why: Establishes the four scope categories that evidence records must use

evidence.summary: Agent prompt requires every evidence record to include scope and requires project facts to have project-scoped evidence with repo-relative paths
evidence.why: Hard-coded requirement in agent prompts enforces scope declaration and path requirements

evidence.summary: When demand explicitly asks for external research, agents must use web/fetch/search-capable tools and mark those records as scope=external
evidence.why: Explicit protocol for external research tool usage and scope marking

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope function classifies records by scope, detecting external URLs, paths, and source types
evidence.why: Implementation of scope classification logic that separates external from project evidence

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues function validates that all evidence records declare scope as project, external, user, or unknown
evidence.why: Validation function that blocks evidence records without proper scope declaration

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues function checks that existing-project facts have project-scoped evidence with concrete path locators
evidence.why: Core validation that prevents external/user/unknown evidence from satisfying project fact requirements

evidence.summary: PRD readiness validation calls evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues for completed evidence results
evidence.why: Integration of scope validation into the PRD readiness gate

evidence.summary: Doctrine requires project evidence must have repo-relative path or file locator; external evidence can only be background research
evidence.why: Product-level specification of scope requirements and evidence path rules

evidence.summary: Dispatch execution allows agents full tools but harness boundary audit detects any target-project mutation
evidence.why: Explains safety model: agents have tool access but boundary audit prevents project file modifications

evidence.summary: Agent prompt includes tool-profile-specific instructions for external research tools, marking records as scope=external
evidence.why: Tool profile awareness ensures external research is properly scoped

risks: External research evidence could be mistaken for project implementation facts if scope validation fails
Agents might not properly distinguish between external documentation and actual project code when providing evidence

result.notes: The implementation already supports the required behavior: evidence-dispatch.ts hard-codes scope requirements in agent prompts (lines 318-320), router.ts implements validation functions (lines 438-447, 513-530), and doctrine specifies the contract (lines 70-71). The system properly separates external research (scope=external) from project facts (scope=project with paths).
